content:
# Original Oil in Place

**Summary**: The total amount of oil in place in the reservoir before any recovery efforts.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2019-alghamdi|AlGhamdi, 2019]] calculated the percentage of original oil in place (OOIP) recovered during core flooding.

## Scientific Context

- [[2019-alghamdi|AlGhamdi, 2019]] OOIP is used as a baseline to measure the effectiveness of EOR methods.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2019-alghamdi|AlGhamdi, 2019]] Calculated from core-flooding data.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The first formulation achieved 62% of the OOIP, while the third reached nearly 70%. ([[2019-alghamdi|AlGhamdi, 2019, p. 18]])
